mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS levels (below 0.25%) reflect an accommodative policy stance that keeps the equity risk premium compressed via low discount rates, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate stays near the zero lower bound.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero-rate periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 5.2% for the first time in a cycle, markets price a higher probability of imminent Fed tightening, which raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as tightening expectations are priced in.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 5.2% for the first time in the current cycle
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.1% as of latest snapshot, near cycle lows preceding expected Fed liftoff
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening ahead of policy normalization
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following an UNRATE sub-5.2% crossing, forward returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS accelerating) combined with a near-zero FEDFUNDS creates a policy-lag mismatch: robust labor data raises the odds of an imminent first rate hike, and the joint occurrence of strong employment plus ultra-low rates produces negative forward S&P 500 returns as markets reprice the hiking path faster than fundamentals justify.
candidate_trigger: 3-month PAYEMS change exceeds +150k while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142,288k with FEDFUNDS at 0.14% ahead of anticipated 2015 liftoff
regime_hint: pre-liftoff monetary policy transition with strong labor momentum
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce positive or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of historical cases, or the interaction term's marginal Rank IC contribution over the single-series baseline is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST (housing starts) during an early-to-mid cycle recovery signals durable consumer demand and construction-driven employment gains, which feed into corporate earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon, but only in regimes where the housing recovery is still underway rather than mature.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,206k units, consistent with ongoing post-recession housing recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST level/trend and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the relationship reverses sign once housing starts exceed pre-recession peak levels (regime shift to late-cycle).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month deceleration in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output, often driven by global demand shocks, and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week as markets adjust earnings expectations for industrial and export-exposed sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO growth softening amid August 2015 global market volatility and China-linked demand concerns
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or negative INDPRO momentum periods show non-negative average forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises above 2%, it raises the probability of accelerated Fed tightening beyond market expectations, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 238.099, currently subdued relative to 2% target
regime_hint: rising inflation environment ahead of policy tightening
failure_condition: Post-trigger 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of historical episodes when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2%, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with a low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly indicate strong consumer-driven demand that is not yet inflationary, supporting continued earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; the effect requires both conditions jointly since either alone is a weaker signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3% while UNRATE remains below 5.5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 447,715 million with UNRATE at 5.1%, indicating solid consumer spending and tight labor market
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with healthy consumer demand
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce negative or flat 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction effect's incremental Rank IC over either single-series proxy is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a pre-liftoff regime where FEDFUNDS has remained near zero for an extended period, incremental hawkish data surprises produce disproportionately large short-term negative equity reactions because markets are highly sensitized to the timing of the first hike, a sensitivity that dissipates once liftoff occurs and normalizes forward-return volatility.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% with markets anticipating imminent first hike in late 2015
regime_hint: pre-liftoff zero-rate regime with heightened policy-timing sensitivity
failure_condition: 5-day forward return volatility and average returns during the pre-liftoff regime are statistically indistinguishable from post-liftoff regime (p > 0.05), or Rank IC in this regime is below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.
